purpose: Give the release operator concrete options after the agent closed compile, critique, adjudication, export, and loop closeout for the current generated export.
evidence: fig-agent status reports render_state=FRESH, critique_state=FRESH, export_state=FRESH, workflow_ready=true, and notes=[]
fig-agent closeout reports closeout_complete=true with compile, critique, adjudication, export, and loop_rerun all passed or not_required
fig-agent loop final next_action_summary reports action=complete and no required plugin action remains
critique.md verdict is ready and critique_lint passed after current detector and crop-manifest refresh
the remaining blocker is release policy only: explicit human acceptance or a valid final-artifact decision
human_question: I recommend accepting the current generated export as the manuscript candidate unless you want a new dogfood/style-redesign pass. Which release decision should I record next?
agent_recommendation: Accept current generated export for this fixture's release state. The current evidence shows no source-level, detector, critique-lint, export, or loop-closeout blocker; remaining improvements are optional style dogfood or future art-direction work.

choices.label: Accept current generated export
state_mutation: record publication/quality audit evidence if required by release policy

follow_up.after_decision: rerun fig-agent status fig3_trapping_concept --json and, if needed, check golden/release readiness

choices.risk: Locks the current restrained mechanism schematic as accepted; later flagship styling becomes a separate improvement, not a blocker.

choices.label: Defer for another dogfood pass

follow_up.after_decision: open a bounded dogfood or style-direction slice without changing accepted state

choices.risk: Release remains blocked even though all current plugin closeout evidence is complete.

choices.label: Request full style redesign

follow_up.after_decision: create redesign alternatives against the current generated export as the benchmark before source mutation

choices.risk: Changes visual language and should not be mixed with release acceptance in the same slice.

choices.label: Declare separate final artifact
state_mutation: add a valid final_artifact declaration to spec.yaml

follow_up.after_decision: verify final_artifact_state becomes FRESH before any release claim

choices.risk: Requires a separate artifact path and freshness proof; it must not silently bless generated output.

choices.label: Reject current artifact
state_mutation: record rejection rationale before any repair or redesign task

follow_up.after_decision: open a bounded repair/redesign task tied to the rejection reason

choices.risk: Rejecting without a concrete defect can reopen completed closeout work.

disallowed_actions: do not treat this packet as implicit human acceptance
do not set accepted: true without explicit release-operator choice
do not mutate source, generated exports, golden artifacts, or final-artifact state from this packet alone
do not start a full redesign in the same slice as acceptance-state mutation

follow_up.implementation_slice: Wait for an explicit release decision, then mutate only the selected acceptance/final-artifact surface and rerun status.
